The text of 40:472(i) is omitted as unnecessary because of the definition of “person” in 1:1.41 U.S.C. 251section 111 of this titleIn clause (6), the words “the Northern Mariana Islands, the Federated States of Micronesia, the Marshall Islands, Palau” are substituted for “the Trust Territory of the Pacific Islands” because of the termination of the Trust Territory of the Pacific Islands. See 48:1681 note prec.

children.label: A
children.text: completing, repairing, converting, rehabilitating, operating, preserving, protecting, insuring, packing, storing, handling, conserving, and transporting excess and surplus property; and

children.label: B
children.text: rendering innocuous, or destroying, property that is dangerous to public health or safety.

children.label: A
children.text: property, in excess of amounts needed to complete full performance, that is acquired by and in possession of a contractor or subcontractor under a contract pursuant to which title is vested in the Federal Government; and

children.label: B
children.text: property that the Government is obligated or has the option to take over, under any type of contract, as a result of changes in specifications or plans under the contract, or as a result of termination of the contract (or a subcontract), prior to completion of the work, for the convenience or at the option of the Government.

children.heading: .—Excess property
children.label: 3 .—Excess property
children.text: The term “excess property” means property under the control of a federal agency that the head of the agency determines is not required to meet the agency’s needs or responsibilities.

children.label: A
children.text: an executive department or independent establishment in the executive branch of the Government; and

children.heading: .—Federal agency
children.label: 5 .—Federal agency
children.text: The term “federal agency” means an executive agency or an establishment in the legislative or judicial branch of the Government (except the Senate, the House of Representatives, and the Architect of the Capitol, and any activities under the direction of the Architect of the Capitol).

children.heading: .—Foreign excess property
children.label: 6 .—Foreign excess property
children.text: The term “foreign excess property” means excess property that is not located in the States of the United States, the District of Columbia, Puerto Rico, American Samoa, Guam, the Northern Mariana Islands, the Federated States of Micronesia, the Marshall Islands, Palau, and the Virgin Islands.

children.label: A
children.text: a vehicle designed or used for military field training, combat, or tactical purposes, or used principally within the confines of a regularly established military post, camp, or depot; and

children.label: B
children.text: a vehicle regularly used by an agency to perform investigative, law enforcement, or intelligence duties, if the head of the agency determines that exclusive control of the vehicle is essential for effective performance of duties.

children.heading: .—Nonpersonal services
children.label: 8 .—Nonpersonal services
children.text: The term “nonpersonal services” means contractual services designated by the Administrator of General Services, other than personal and professional services.

children.label: ii
children.text: land reserved or dedicated for national forest or national park purposes;

children.label: iii
children.text: minerals in land or portions of land withdrawn or reserved from the public domain which the Secretary of the Interior determines are suitable for disposition under the public land mining and mineral leasing laws; and

children.label: iv
children.text: land withdrawn or reserved from the public domain except land or portions of land so withdrawn or reserved which the Secretary, with the concurrence of the Administrator, determines are not suitable for return to the public domain for disposition under the general public land laws because the lands are substantially changed in character by improvements or otherwise;

children.label: B
children.text: naval vessels that are battleships, cruisers, aircraft carriers, destroyers, or submarines; and

children.heading: .—Surplus property
children.label: 10 .—Surplus property
children.text: The term “surplus property” means excess property that the Administrator determines is not required to meet the needs or responsibilities of all federal agencies.
